chief_complaint: A 40-year-old woman presented with recurrent per rectal bleeding and haemodynamic instability.
history: The patient presented with recurrent per rectal bleeding and haemodynamic instability. Extensive investigations including upper and lower endoscopy, capsule endoscopy, nuclear scintigraphy and CT angiography failed to identify a definitive bleeding source. Stool polymerase chain reaction (PCR) confirmed Shiga toxin 2-producing Escherichia coli.
exam: Vital signs: unstable. Physical examination: not specified.
labs: Stool PCR: Shiga toxin 2-producing Escherichia coli. Scintigraphy: active bleeding near the caecum. CT angiogram: faint blush in the ascending colon.
